issues.detail: Your page title tag is missing. This appears in search results and browser tabs.
issues.label: Missing page title
issues.check: Open the page. Look at the browser tab text. If it says the URL or 'Home' instead of your business name, it is missing.
issues.siteHint: Google may show your URL instead of a proper title in search results.

issues.detail: H1 headings tell Google the main topic of your page.
issues.label: No H1 heading
issues.check: View page source (Ctrl+U). Search for an H1 tag. If there is none, the page structure is weak.
issues.siteHint: Missing H1 weakens your search ranking signals.

issues.detail: Schema helps Google show your hours, reviews, and events in search results.
issues.label: No schema markup
issues.check: Search for your business on Google. Does it show a Knowledge Panel with hours and reviews?
issues.siteHint: Detailed search results get higher click-through rates.

issues.detail: HTTP Strict Transport Security tells browsers to always use HTTPS. Without it, a downgrade attack is possible.
issues.label: Missing HSTS header
issues.check: Open DevTools ÔåÆ Network ÔåÆ click the first request ÔåÆ look for "strict-transport-security".
issues.siteHint: HSTS is a simple config that hardens your site against protocol downgrade attacks.

issues.detail: CSP helps prevent XSS attacks by controlling which resources can load.
issues.label: Missing Content Security Policy
issues.check: Check DevTools Console for CSP-related warnings.
issues.siteHint: A basic CSP blocks most common script injection attacks.

issues.detail: Without this, your site could be embedded in an iframe on another site (clickjacking risk).
issues.label: Missing X-Frame-Options header
issues.check: Check if the site has X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN in response headers.
issues.siteHint: Without this, others could embed your site on malicious pages.

issues.label: Missing X-Content-Type-Options header
issues.check: Check DevTools Network tab for the header.

issues.detail: The homepage lacks a contact form. There is one on /contact, but visitors reaching the homepage may not find it.
issues.label: Contact form hidden on subpage
issues.check: Look at the top-level navigation. Is "Contact" easy to find from the homepage?
issues.siteHint: A visible contact form on the main page can increase inquiries by 30-50%. Visitors should not have to hunt for it.

positives.detail: SSL certificate valid ÔÇö 96 days remaining

observations.label: Menu text blends into background
observations.detail: The body text on the menu page uses a colour too close to the background shade. You literally have to squint to read it. On mobile in sunlight it's borderline unusable.
observations.suggestion: Darken the body text to near-black (#1a202c or #333) against the light background. Avoid grey-on-grey combos for anything that carries information.

observations.label: Welcome blurb is a wall of fluff — no practical info
observations.detail: The homepage opens with three dense paragraphs about 'warm hospitality and culinary delights' before telling customers anything useful. Visitors scanning the page need to find opening hours, booking info, and any closure warnings immediately — not after scrolling past marketing copy.
observations.suggestion: Put opening hours and a booking CTA at the very top of the page. Move the descriptive blurb below the practical info. Add a visible notice banner for closures or special hours.

observations.label: Menu categories blend together on the page
observations.detail: The sections (Starters, Main Courses) don't visually separate from the items below them — it's just one long scroll of text. Hard to tell where one category ends and the next begins. A border or different background on each section would help diners navigate.
observations.suggestion: Wrap each menu category (Starters, Mains, Desserts, etc) in a card with a light background and subtle border. Make the category headings bold and visually distinct with a background colour.
